mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easing financial conditions that lift equity multiples and reduce discount rates, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is declining on a month-over-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns during months when FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising rapidly while FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound, real rates remain deeply negative and inflation uncertainty compresses risk premia; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by at least 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and increases recession odds; this should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after a clear unemployment deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-shock windows, or if the signal has no explanatory power relative to a zero-change benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad demand growth and improving firm revenue visibility, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with simultaneous positive 3-month changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform the unconditional 60-day return distribution, or if one proxy alone explains the effect better than the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and inventory normalization, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO upswings are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals a sharp housing demand slowdown that typically precedes weaker consumption and earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days after a sustained housing downturn.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing downturn episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the drawdown signal does not improve forecast accuracy versus random timing.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is accelerating, the economy is simultaneously expanding in output and nominal spending, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-sales expansion signal fails to outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive when both series are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, a further rise in UNRATE confirms recessionary labor weakness and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also deteriorating.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-stress signal does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns specifically when UNRATE is above its recent elevated level and PAYEMS is falling, or if the conditional hit rate is below 50%.
